name: Digital
description_short: DCU is a member-owned credit union offering checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and mortgages. Recently merged with First Technology Federal Credit Union to expand services.
description_long:
DCU (Digital Credit Union) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union regulated by the NCUA. The organization recently completed a merger with First Technology Federal Credit Union, combining operations to serve a broader membership base across personal and business banking. This merger represents a significant expansion of DCU's service capacity and member base.

DCU offers comprehensive financial products including free checking accounts (including HSA Checking), multiple savings account types (Primary, Advantage, Smart, Money Market), certificates of deposit, and IRAs. On the lending side, they provide auto loans (including refinancing and specialty vehicles like motorcycles and RVs), personal loans, credit cards (Visa Platinum, Signature Cash Rewards, Secured options), student loans, mortgages with refinancing options, home equity loans, and commercial lending for small businesses and real estate. They also offer specialty accounts like Coverdell Education Savings and trust accounts.

DCU differentiates itself through member-focused pricing with no-fee checking accounts and an emphasis on helping members "stop paying big bank fees." They provide free services including monthly FICO score access, text alerts, eStatements, notary services, and signature guarantees. The credit union offers online calculators for vehicles, savings, loans, mortgages, and retirement planning. They also provide skip-a-payment programs, GAP Advantage insurance, and mechanical breakdown insurance on auto loans.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, DCU operates on a not-for-profit basis and returns profits to members rather than shareholders. The recent First Tech merger is still integrating operations, with First Tech members continuing to manage accounts through their existing website during the transition. This presents a potential consideration for prospective members regarding system integration and service consistency during the consolidation period.
services: Free Checking Accounts and HSA Checking
Multiple Savings Account types (Primary, Advantage, Smart, Money Market)
Retirement Accounts (Certificate IRA, Money Market IRA, Savings IRA)
Auto Loans and Auto Refinancing (including specialty vehicles)
Personal Loans and Credit Builder Loans
Visa Credit Cards (Platinum, Signature Cash Rewards, Secured)
Student Loans and Student Loan Refinancing
Business Checking and Business Savings Accounts
Commercial Lending (Small Business, Commercial Real Estate, C&I Loans)
pros: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees, positioning members to avoid traditional bank charges
Free monthly FICO score access bundled with membership, helping members monitor credit without additional cost
Diverse loan portfolio including specialty auto loans (antique cars, motorcycles, RVs, boats) not commonly offered by traditional banks
Comprehensive online calculators for vehicles, savings, loans, mortgages, and retirement planning to aid financial decisions
Free notary and signature guarantee services for members
Skip-a-payment programs and insurance options (GAP Advantage, Mechanical Breakdown) on auto loans
Not-for-profit structure returns profits to members rather than shareholders
cons: Recent merger with First Technology Federal Credit Union means systems are still integrating; First Tech members must continue using their separate website during transition, creating temporary inconvenience
Limited information on membership eligibility requirements and geographic service area constraints typical of credit unions
No specific APR or rate information provided on website, requiring members to contact directly for pricing details
Availability of premium services like skip-a-payment programs and specialized insurance suggests some services may have additional costs or eligibility restrictions not fully disclosed
Merger integration period may create service delays or inconsistencies during the operational combination phase
best_for: Members seeking to avoid big bank fees through free checking and savings products
Auto loan borrowers with specialty vehicle needs (motorcycles, RVs, boats, antique cars) underserved by traditional banks
Consumers wanting free credit monitoring and financial planning tools without premium service fees
Small business owners seeking member-owned lending alternatives and commercial banking services
diagnosis: DCU is best for credit union members prioritizing fee-free banking, free credit monitoring, and access to specialty auto loans. The primary caveat is that the recent First Tech merger is still integrating operations, meaning prospective members should verify current service capabilities and confirm membership eligibility before applying, as the consolidation period may affect service delivery and product availability.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Digital — Credit Union in Marlborough, MA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Digital — NCUA-insured credit union in Marlborough, MA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
